zh.lifeSummary: 當Alexander Carragh MacDonald of Islay於1480年出生在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾時，他的父親John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens31歲，母親Cecilia Savage16歲。他於大約1498年與Catherine MacIain of Ardnamurchan在蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1538年在蘇格蘭斯林史爾斯特林享年58歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alexander Carragh MacDonald of Islay于1480年出生在苏格兰音巴內-爾时，他的父亲John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens31岁，母亲Cecilia Savage16岁。他于大约1498年与Catherine MacIain of Ardnamurchan在苏格兰阿吉爾结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1538年在苏格兰斯林史爾斯特林享年58岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: MacDonald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: McDonald
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Domhnaill ‘son of Domhnall’, a name derived from the Celtic elements domno- ‘world’ + val- ‘might, rule’. Donald is an Anglicized form (via Latin) of this personal name used in Scotland, though the surname is also widespread in Ireland. The name is equivalent to Irish McDonnell and McConnell , and to Manx Cannell .

History: This is the name of the largest and most disparate of the Scottish clans (Clan Donald), associated in particular with the Hebrides and claiming descent from Domhnall mac Raghnaill mac Somhairle, who lived in the late 12th century. From that time until 1493 the head of the clan was known as Lord (or King) of the Isles. The reigns of the Lords of the Isles were always stormy, often in conflict with the kings of Scotland, and peppered with disasters. After a series of defeats in the 1480s, Eoin Mac Dhomhnaill a Ìle (John Macdonald of Islay, Earl of Ross and Lord of the Isles) forfeited his lands, his power, and his title as Lord of the Isles in 1493 to King James IV of Scotland. By then, a branch of the family had settled in the Antrim Glens in Ireland and members moved between the southern Hebrides and Ireland throughout the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

nameMeanings.name: Carragh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: popularly associated with the Scottish Gaelic vocabulary word darach ‘oak’ (Irish dair, genitive darach). As an Irish name it also functions as an Anglicized form of Dáire . It is one of the Celtic names that is now spreading into more general use.

variants.name: Darach
meanings: popularly associated with the Scottish Gaelic vocabulary word darach ‘oak’ (Irish dair, genitive darach). As an Irish name it also functions as an Anglicized form of Dáire . It is one of the Celtic names that is now spreading into more general use.

variants.name: Daragh
meanings: popularly associated with the Scottish Gaelic vocabulary word darach ‘oak’ (Irish dair, genitive darach). As an Irish name it also functions as an Anglicized form of Dáire . It is one of the Celtic names that is now spreading into more general use.

variants.name: Dara
meanings: short form of Mac Dara ‘son of oak’ . This name, though still particularly associated with Connemara, is now found throughout Ireland and elsewhere in the English-speaking world. It was formerly often Anglicized as   Dudley .

en.lifeSummary: When Alexander Carragh MacDonald of Islay was born in 1480, in Inverness-shire, Scotland, his father, John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens, was 31 and his mother, Cecilia Savage, was 16. He married Catherine MacIain of Ardnamurchan about 1498, in Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 3 daughters. He died in 1538, in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, at the age of 58, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alexander Carragh MacDonald of Islay est né en 1480, à Inverness-shire, Écosse, son père, John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Cecilia Savage, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Catherine MacIain of Ardnamurchan environ 1498, à Argyll, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé en 1538, à Stirling, Stirlingshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alexander Carragh MacDonald of Islay 1480 in Inverness-shire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens 31 und seine Mutter Cecilia Savage 16. Er hat um 1498 in Argyll, Scotland Catherine MacIain of Ardnamurchan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1538 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alexander Carragh MacDonald of Islay è nato il nel 1480 nel Inverness-shire, Scotland, il suo padre, John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Cecilia Savage, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Catherine MacIain of Ardnamurchan circa 1498, nel Argyll, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1538, nel Stirling, Stirlingshire, Scotland, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ko.lifeSummary: Alexander Carragh MacDonald of Islay(이)가 1480년 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Cecilia Savage(은)는 16세였다. 그는 대략 1498년 스컷랜드 아그이에서 Catherine MacIain of Ardnamurchan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1538년 스컷랜드 스티를링실르 스티를링에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alexander Carragh MacDonald of Islay nasceu em 1480, em Inverness-shire, Scotland, seu pai, John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Cecilia Savage, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Catherine MacIain of Ardnamurchan aproximadamente 1498, em Argyll, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.Dunnyveg, Islay, Western Isles, Scotland: Comhairle nan Eilean Siar, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alexander Carragh MacDonald of Islay родился в 1480 г. в местности инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени John Cathanach MacDonald of Dunnyveg and the Glens было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Cecilia Savage, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine MacIain of Ardnamurchan около 1498 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер в 1538 г. в местности стирлинг, стирлингшир, Шотландия в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.
